title: Proclamation No. 1354, s. 1974
text:
MALACAÑANG Manila

PROCLAMATION NO. 1354

EXCLUDING FROM THE OPERATION OF PROCLAMATION NO. 453 DATED DECEMBER 23, 1953, WHICH ESTABLISHED THE CAMP KEITHLEY (NOW CAMP AMAI PAKPAK) MILITARY RESERVATION, SITUATED IN THE CITY OF MARAWI, ISLAND OF MINDANAO, A CERTAIN PORTION OF THE LAND EMBRACED THEREIN AND RESERVING THE SAME FOR THE HYDROELECTRIC PROJECTS OF THE NATIONAL POWER CORPORATION

By virtue of the powers vested in me by law, I, FERDINAND E. MARCOS, President of the Republic of the Philippines, do hereby exclude from the operation of Proclamation No. 453 of December 23, 1953, which established the Camp Keithley (now Camp Amai Pakpak) Military Reservation, situated in the City of Marawi, Island of Mindanao, a portion of land embraced therein and reserve the same for hydroelectric power purposes under the administration, supervision and control of the National Power Corporation, subject to private rights, if any there be, which portion of land is more particularly described and bounded as follows:

A parcel of land (Lot A portion of military reservation) situated at Barrio Lumbayanague, Municipality of Saguiran, Province of Lanao del Sur, Island of Mindanao, bounded on the North along line 1-2 and 1-46 by public land, in the East by the Military Reservation, on the South by public land and on the West by the Military Reservation. Beginning at a point marked I (USMR-1) on plan being N 62º 39’E., 3,937.834 meters from point 4 of the Military Reservation of Camp Amai Pakpak (formerly Camp Keithley), Marawi City;

thence S 62º 39’W,   895.56 m. to point “2” thence S 30º 13’E,  91.88 m. to point “3” thence S 28º 24’E,  101.79 m. to point “4” thence S 20º 56’E,  107.90 m. to point “5” thence S 50º 06’E,  183.33 m. to point “6” thence S 49º 17’E,  41.64 m. to point “7” thence S 48º 32’E,  98.93 m. to point “8” thence S 47º 14’E,  144.03 m. to point “9” thence S 36º 56’E,  143.80 m. to point “10” thence S 37º 47’E,  99.78 m. to point “11” thence S 05º 02’E,  5,544.42 m. to point “12” thence N 51º 44’E,  1,619.73 m. to point “13” thence N 42º 04’W,  43.72 m. to point “14” thence N 03º 38’W,  45.51 m. to point “15” thence S 84º 15’W,  26.31 m. to point “16” thence N 19º 12’W,  28.66 m. to point “17” thence N 25º 05’W,  26.95 m. to point “18” thence N 78º 52’W,  27.44 m. to point “19” thence N 39º 46’W,  43.37 m. to point “20” thence N 66º 34’W,  34.81 m. to point “21” thence N 53º 09’W,  47.87 m. to point “22” thence N 06º 51’W,  48.00 m. to point “23” thence N 13º 04’W,  40.63 m. to point “24” thence N 08º 44’E,  49.11 m. to point “25” thence N 28º 42’W,  45.72 m. to point “26” thence N 26º 34’W,  95.75 m. to point “27” thence N 72º 05’W,  96.98 m. to point “28” thence N 62º 04’W,  81.84 m. to point “29” thence S 88º 40’W,  31.25 m. to point “30” thence N 55º 37’W,  49.13 m. to point “31” thence N 79º 37’W,  72.70 m. to point “32” thence N 37º 41’W,  126.31 m. to point “33” thence N 06º 30’E,  43.86 m. to point “34” thence N 72º 08’W,  43.86 m. to point “35” thence S 83º 40’W,  62.02 m. to point “36” thence N 16º 30’E,  250.00 m. to point “37” thence N 21º 51’W,  1,568.34 m. to point “38” thence N 29º 30’E,  310.00 m. to point “39” thence S 84º 10’E,  2,100.00 m. to point “40” thence N 07º 40’E,  500.00 m. to point “41” thence N 44º 22’W,  2,689.03 m. to point “42” thence N 26º 09’W,  246.24 m. to point “43” thence N 24º 28’W,  251.51 m. to point “44” thence N 25º 52’W,  326.29 m. to point “45” thence N 32º 49’W,  234.21 m. to point “46” thence S 62º 39’W,  304.44 m. to point of beginning; containing an area of EIGHT MILLION THIRTY-ONE THOUSAND FOUR HUNDRED FORTY (8,031,440) SQUARE METERS, more or less. All points referred to are indicated on plan and marked on the ground by NPC Cylindrical concrete monuments (15×60 cm.) in diameter. It was surveyed on November 7, 1973 to December 23, 1973.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, I have hereunto set my hand and caused the seal of the Republic of the Philippines to be affixed.

Done in the City of Manila, this 3rd day of December, in the year of Our Lord, nineteen hundred and seventy-four.

(SGD.) FERDINAND E. MARCOS President Republic of the Philippines

By the President:

(SGD.) ALEJANDRO MELCHOR Executive Secretary

Source: CDAsia
